---
title: "MultiSim: Parallel Runs and Uncertainty (Python)"
author: "Simon Frost"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    toc: true
  gfm:
    toc: true
engine: python
---

## Overview

This is the Python companion to the Julia `12_multisim` vignette. We run
multiple stochastic replications of an SIR simulation and aggregate results.

## Basic MultiSim

```{python}
import starsim as ss
import numpy as np
import pylab as pl

n_contacts = 10
beta = 0.5 / n_contacts

sim = ss.Sim(
    n_agents=1000,
    networks=ss.RandomNet(n_contacts=n_contacts),
    diseases=ss.SIR(beta=beta, dur_inf=4, init_prev=0.01),
    dt=1.0,
    start=0,
    stop=40,
    rand_seed=42,
    verbose=0,
)

msim = ss.MultiSim(sim, n_runs=10)
msim.run(verbose=0)
```

## Reduced results

```{python}
msim.reduce()

# After reduce, results are flattened: sir_prevalence, etc.
import pylab as pl

prev = msim.results.sir_prevalence
tvec = np.arange(len(prev.values))

pl.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
pl.fill_between(tvec, prev.low, prev.high,
                alpha=0.3, color='red', label='10th–90th percentile')
pl.plot(tvec, prev.values, color='darkred', lw=2, label='Median')
pl.xlabel('Day')
pl.ylabel('Prevalence')
pl.title('SIR Prevalence — MultiSim (10 runs)')
pl.legend()
pl.show()
```

## Mean-based reduction

```{python}
msim.mean()

prev = msim.results.sir_prevalence
tvec = np.arange(len(prev.values))

pl.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
pl.fill_between(tvec, prev.low, prev.high,
                alpha=0.3, color='blue', label='Mean ± 2σ')
pl.plot(tvec, prev.values, color='darkblue', lw=2, label='Mean')
pl.xlabel('Day')
pl.ylabel('Prevalence')
pl.title('SIR Prevalence — Mean ± 2σ')
pl.legend()
pl.show()

peak_prev = max(prev.values)
print(f"Mean peak prevalence: {peak_prev:.4f}")
```
